title: CallLint Artifact Identity
description: A digest-pinned identity (calllint.artifact.v1) for the thing a Trust Gateway run evaluates. Pins WHAT is being evaluated: source, the immutable resolved ref, and the digest of fetched bytes. A mutable requestedRef (branch/tag/latest/range) MUST be pinned to an immutable resolvedRef before the state machine may leave RESOLVED. This kills branch/version drift and is the anchor every downstream object (Evidence, Authority, Decision, Plan, Receipt) binds. See ADR 0035.

sourceType.description: How the target was located. git/npm are remote (need network to pin); dir/file/mcp-config are local (offline).

source.description: The user-supplied locator, verbatim (git URL, directory path, file path, npm spec).

requestedRef.description: What the user asked for (e.g. "main", "^1.0.0", or null for a local path). May be mutable.

resolvedRef.description: The immutable ref: a git commit sha, an exact npm version, or a content marker for local paths. REQUIRED (non-null) before the artifact is considered FETCHED. null MUST coincide with resolution != "resolved" and a resolutionReasons entry.

digest.description: sha256 of the fetched bytes (a stable tree hash for git/dir, file bytes for file, the resolved manifest for mcp-config). null MUST coincide with resolution != "resolved".

resolvedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, injected from the CLI edge (never Date.now() in pure core).

resolution.description: Whether the artifact was pinned to an immutable, digested identity. "resolved" = resolvedRef + digest present. "partial"/"unresolved" = could not fully pin (e.g. offline remote target); MUST NOT read as a verified target and blocks leaving RESOLVED.

resolutionReasons.description: Why the artifact is not fully resolved (offline, network error, ambiguous ref, missing file). Non-empty REQUIRES resolution in {partial, unresolved}.
